solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string multiply(string num1, string num2) {
        if (num1 == "0" || num2 == "0") return "0";
        
        vector<int> result(num1.size() + num2.size(), 0);
        
        for (int i = num1.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
            for (int j = num2.size() - 1; j >= 0; --j) {
                int mul = (num1[i] - '0') * (num2[j] - '0');
                int sum = mul + result[i + j + 1];
                result[i + j + 1] = sum % 10;
                result[i + j] += sum / 10;
            }
        }
        
        string output;
        int i = 0;
        while (i < result.size() && result[i] == 0) ++i;
        while (i < result.size()) {
            output.push_back('0' + result[i++]);
        }
        return output;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N * M)
space complexity: O(N + M)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution simulates the multiplication process manually, digit by digit, storing intermediate results in an array. It avoids using any built-in large integer libraries and handles the multiplication efficiently by leveraging the properties of positional arithmetic. The time complexity is O(N * M) due to the nested loops over the digits of the two numbers, and the space complexity is O(N + M) for the result array.
